author: śrī⋅vedavyāsa
verses:
śrī⋅rudra uvāca
jitaṁ ta ātmavid⋅varya⋅svastaye svastirastu me
bhabatārādhasā rāddhaṁ sarvasmā ātmane namaḥ

namaḥ paṅkaja⋅nābhāya bhūta⋅sūkṣmendriyātmane
vāsudevāya śāntāya kūṭasthāya svarociṣe

saṅkarṣaṇāya sūkṣmāya durantāyāntakāya ca
namo viśva⋅prabodhāya pradyumnāyāntarātmane

namo namo‛niruddhāya hṛṣīkeśendriyātmane
namaḥ parama⋅haṁsāya pūrṇāya nibhṛtātmane

svargāpavarga⋅dvārāya nityaṁ śuciṣade namaḥ
namo hiraṇya⋅vīryāya cāturhotrāya tantave

nama ūrja iṣe trayyāḥ pataye jajña⋅retase
tṛptidāya ca jīvānāṁ namaḥ sarva⋅rasātmane

sarva⋅sattvātma⋅dehāya viśeṣāya sthavīyase
namastrailokya⋅pālāya saha ojo⋅balāya ca

artha⋅liṅgāya nabhase namo‛ntarbahirātmane
namaḥ puṇyāya lokāya amusmai bhūrivarcase

pravṛttāya nivṛttāya pitṛ⋅devāya karmaṇe
namo‛dharma⋅vipākāya mṛtyave duḥkhadāya ca

namasta āśīṣāmīśa manave kāraṇātmane
namo dharmāya bṛhate kṛṣṇāyākuṇṭha⋅medhase
puruṣāya purāṇāya sāṅkhya⋅yogeśvarāya ca

śakti⋅traya⋅sametāya mīḍhuṣe‛haṅkṛtātmane
ceta⋅ākṛti⋅rūpāya namo vāco vibhūtaye

darśanaṁ no didṛkṣūṇāṁ dehi bhāgavatārcitam
rūpaṁ priyatamaṁ svānāṁ sarvendriya⋅guṇaañjanam

snigdha⋅prāvṛḍghana⋅śyāmaṁ sarva⋅saundarya⋅saŋgraham
cārvāyata⋅caturbāhu sujāta⋅rucirānanam
padma⋅kośa⋅palāśākṣaṁ sundara⋅bhru sunāsikam
sudvijaṁ sukapolāsyaṁ sama⋅karṇa⋅vibhūṣaṇam

prīti⋅prahasitāpāṅgamalakai rūpa⋅śobhitam
lasat⋅paṅkaja⋅kiñjalka⋅dukūlaṁ mṛṣṭa⋅kuṇḍalam
sphurat⋅kirīṭa⋅valaya⋅hāra⋅nūpura⋅mekhalam
śaṅkha⋅cakra⋅gadā⋅padma⋅mālā⋅maṇyuttamardhimat

siŋha⋅skandha⋅tviṣo bibhratsaubhaga⋅grīva⋅kaustubham
śriyānapāyinyā kṣipta⋅nikaṣāśmorasollasat

pūra⋅recaka⋅saṁvigna⋅bali⋅valgudalodaram
pratisaŋkrāmayadviśvaṁ nābhyāvarta⋅gabhīrayā

śyāma⋅śroṇyadhirociṣṇu⋅dukūla⋅svarṇa⋅mekhalam
sama⋅cārvaṅghri⋅jaṅghoru⋅nimna⋅jānu⋅sudarśanam

padā śarat⋅padma⋅palāśa⋅rociṣā
nakha⋅dyubhirno‛ntaraghaṁ vidhunvatā
pradarśaya svīyamapāsta⋅sādhvasaṁ
padaṁ guro mārga⋅gurustamojuṣām

etad⋅rūpamanudhyeyamātma⋅śuddhimabhīpsatām
jad⋅bhakti⋅yogo‛bhayadaḥ svadharmamanutiṣṭhatām

bhabān bhakti⋅matā labhyo durlabhaḥ sarva⋅dehinām
svārājyasyāpyabhimata ekāntenātmavid⋅gatiḥ

taṁ durārādhyamārādhya satāmapi durāpayā
ekānta⋅bhaktyā ko vāñchet⋅pāda⋅mūlaṁ vinā bahiḥ

jatra niviṣṭa⋅maraṇaṁ kṛtānto nābhimanyate
viśvaṁ vidhvaṁsayan vīrya⋅śaurya⋅visphūrjita⋅bhruvā

kṣaṇārdhenāpi tulaye na svargaṁ nāpunarbhavam
bhagavat⋅saṅgi⋅saṅgasya martyānāṁ kimutāśīṣaḥ

athānaghāṅghrestava kīrti⋅tīrthayo⋅
rantarbahiḥ⋅snāna⋅vidhūta⋅pāpmanām
bhūteṣbanukrośa⋅susattva⋅śīlināṁ
syāt⋅saṅgamo‛nugraha eṣa nastava

na jasya cittaṁ bahirartha⋅vibhramaṁ
tamoguhāyāṁ ca viśuddhamāviśat
jad⋅bhakti⋅yogānugṛhītamañjasā
munirrvicaṣṭe nanu tatra te gatim

jatredaṁ vyajyate viśvaṁ viśvasminnavabhāti jat
tat tvaṁ brahma paraṁ jyotirākāśamiva vistṛtam

jo māyayedaṁ puru⋅rūpayāsṛjad
bibharti bhūyaḥ kṣapayatyavikriyaḥ
jad⋅bhadra⋅buddhiḥ sadivātmaduḥsthayā
tvamātma⋅tantraṁ bhagavan pratīmahi

kriyā⋅kalāpairidameva joginaḥ
śraddhānvitāḥ sādhu jajanti siddhaye
bhūtendriyā antaḥkaraṇopalakṣitaṁ
vede ca tantre ca ta eva kovidāḥ

tvameka ādyaḥ puruṣaḥ supta⋅śakti⋅
stayā rajaḥ⋅sattva⋅tamo vibhidyate
mahānahaṁ khaṁ marudgnibārdharāḥ
surarṣayo bhūta⋅gaṇā idaṁ jataḥ

sṛṣṭaṁ sva⋅śaktyedamanupraviṣṭa⋅
ścacaturvidhaṁ puramātmāṁśakena
atho vidustaṁ puruṣaṁ santamanta⋅
rbhṛṅkte hṛṣīkairmadhu sāraghaṁ jaḥ

sa eṣa lokānaticaṇḍa⋅vego
vikarṣasi tvaṁ khalu kālayānaḥ
bhūtāni bhūtairanumeya⋅tattvo
ghanāvalī⋅vāyurivaaviṣahyaḥ

pravṛddhamuccairiti kṛtya⋅cintayā
pravṛddha⋅lobhaṁ viṣayeṣu lālasam
tvamapramattaḥ sahasābhipadyase
kṣullelihāno‛hirivākṣumantakaḥ

kastvat⋅padābjaṁ vijahāti paṇḍito
jaste‛vamāna⋅vyayamāna⋅ketanaḥ
viśaṅkayāsmada⋅gururarcati sma jad
vinopapattiṁ manavaścaturdaśa

atha tvamasi no brahman paramātman vipaścitām
viśvaṁ rudra⋅bhaya⋅dhvastamakutaścidbhayā gatiḥ

idaṁ japata bhadraṁ vo viśuddhā nṛpa⋅nandanāḥ
svadharmamanutiṣṭhanto bhagavatyarpitāśayāḥ

tamevātmānamātmasthaṁ sarva⋅bhūteṣvavasthitam
pūjayadhvaṁ gṛṇantaśca dhyāyantaścāsakṛddharim

jogādeśamupāsādya dhārayanto muni⋅vratāḥ
samāhita⋅dhiyaḥ sarva etadabhyasatādṛtāḥ

idamāha purāsmākaṁ bhagavān viśvasṛkpatiḥ
bhṛgbādīnāmātmajānāṁ sisṛkṣuḥ saṁsisṛkṣatām

te vayaṁ noditāḥ sarve prajā⋅sarge prajeśvarāḥ
anena dhvasta⋅tamasaḥ sisṛkṣmo vividhāḥ prajāḥ

athaidaṁ nityadā jukto japannavahitaḥ pumān
acirācchreya āpnoti vāsudeva⋅parāyaṇaḥ

śreyasāmiha sarveṣāṁ jñānaṁ niḥśreyasaṁ param
sukhaṁ tarati duṣpāraṁ jñāna⋅naur⋅vyasanārṇavam

ja imaṁ śraddhayā jukto mad⋅gītaṁ bhagavat⋅stavam
adhīyāno durārādhyaṁ harimārādhayatyasau

vindate puruṣo‛musmād jad jadicchatyasatvaram
mad⋅gīta⋅gītāt⋅suprītāt śreyasāmeka⋅vallabhāt

idaṁ jaḥ kalya utthāya prāñjaliḥ śraddhayāmvitaḥ
śṛṇuyācchrāvayenmartyo mucyate karma⋅bandhanaiḥ

gītaṁ mayedaṁ naradeva⋅nandanāḥ
parasya puṁsaḥ paramātmanaḥ stavam
japanta ekāgra⋅dhiyastapo mahat
caradhvamante tata āpsyathepsitam
en_translation:
Śrī Rudra said: O Best of the Self-Realized! Victory to You! O Self of all, let there be auspiciousness for me. I offer my obeisances to You, who are served by You.

Obeisances to the Lotus-naveled One, the Soul of the subtle elements and senses. To Vāsudeva, the peaceful, the unchanging, the self-effulgent.

To Saṅkarṣaṇa, the subtle, the endless, and the destroyer. Obeisances to the awakener of the universe, Pradyumna, the inner soul.

Obeisances, obeisances to Aniruddha, the Master of the senses, the Soul of the senses. Obeisances to the Paramahaṁsa, the Full, the concealed Soul.

Obeisances to Him who is the door to heaven and liberation, who always dwells in the pure heart. Obeisances to Hiraṇya-vīrya, the expander of the Cātur-hotra sacrifice.

Obeisances to Ūrja (strength), Iṣa (food), the Lord of the three Vedas, the source of sacrifice. To the giver of satisfaction to the jīvas, obeisances to the Soul of all rasas.

To Him whose body is all-existence, the Specific, the Grossest. Obeisances to the Protector of the three worlds, along with Ojas (vitality) and Bala (strength).

To the Meaning-gendered, the Sky, obeisances to the Inner and Outer Soul. Obeisances to the Pious World, to that One of great splendor.

To the active, the renounced, the Worshipable Deity of the forefathers, to Karma. Obeisances to the result of irreligion, to Death, and to the giver of misery.

Obeisances to You, the Lord of benedictions, to Manu, to the Causal Soul. Obeisances to Dharma, the Great, to Kṛṣṇa of stunted intelligence. To the Person, the Ancient, the Lord of Sāṅkhya and Yoga.

To Him endowed with the three energies, to the Showerer, to the Ego-soul. To the form of Consciousness, obeisances to the manifestation of Speech.

Give us, who are eager to see, the vision worshipped by the devotees. The form most dear to Your own, the collyrium for all the senses and qualities.

Dark like a glistening raincloud, the reservoir of all beauty. With four beautiful long arms, and a well-formed, charming face. Eyes like lotus petals, beautiful eyebrows, beautiful nose. Beautiful teeth, beautiful cheeks and face, adorned with equal earrings.

Beautified by sidelong glances laughing with love and by locks of hair. With a garment like the filaments of a shining lotus, and polished earrings. With shining crown, bracelets, necklaces, anklets, and girdle. Endowed with the excellent opulence of conch, disc, club, lotus, garland, and jewels.

Bearing the Kaustubha on a beautiful neck, with the shoulders of a lion. Shining on the chest, defeating the touchstone with the never-departing Śrī (Lakṣmī).

With an abdomen beautiful with folds agitated by breathing in and out. Deep with a navel-whirlpool that seems to draw the universe back in.

With a golden belt on a shining cloth over dark hips. With symmetrical, beautiful feet, shins, thighs, and knees—beautiful to see.

By Your feet, which have the luster of autumn lotus petals, and by the light of Your nails which destroys our inner sin. Show us Your own feet, which remove fear, O Guru! You are the Guru of the path for those in darkness.

This form should be meditated upon by those desiring self-purification. Whose bhakti-yoga grants fearlessness to those performing their own duty.

You are attainable by one who has devotion, but rare for all embodied beings. Even for the King of Heaven, the goal of the knowers of the Self is desired in solitude.

Having worshipped Him who is difficult to worship, with unalloyed devotion rare even for the saintly. Who would desire anything outside, without the root of Your feet?

Where death does not think to enter, absorbed. Destroying the universe with eyebrows flashing with heroism and valor.

I do not weigh heaven or liberation against even half a moment of the association of those attached to the Lord. What to speak of the benedictions of mortals?

Therefore, let us have the association of those whose sins have been washed away by bathing internally and externally in the holy rivers of Your glories and Your sinless feet. Who are endowed with compassion and good character towards living beings. This is Your mercy upon us.

Whose mind, bewildered by external objects, does not enter the cave of darkness, being purified. By whose bhakti-yoga, graced easily, the sage sees Your path there.

Where this universe is manifested, which shines in the universe. You are that Brahman, the Supreme Light, expanded like the sky.

Who created this with the Maya of many forms, maintains it again, and destroys it, being unchanging. By whom the intelligence is different, as if existing, due to the bad state of the self. We understand You, the Lord, to be independent.

With these activities, the yogis endowed with faith worship this very thing perfectly for perfection. Characterized by the elements, senses, and inner instrument, in the Veda and Tantra, they alone are the knowers.

You are the One, the Original Person, whose energy is asleep. By that, passion, goodness, and ignorance are divided. From whom came this: the Mahat, the Ego, the Sky, Air, Fire, Water, Earth, the Suras, Sages, and hosts of beings.

Created by Your own energy, having entered this fourfold city with a portion of Yourself. Then they know that Person to be existing within, who enjoys the honey made by bees with the senses.

He, this one, of very fierce speed, You pull the worlds, driving Time. Beings by beings, the Truth inferred, like the wind the clouds, unbearable.

Thinking loudly "This is to be done," greedy, lusty for objects. You, always alert, suddenly seize the madman, like a hungry snake a mouse licking its lips.

What learned person abandons Your lotus feet, when You remove the banner of disrespect? Out of fear, our Guru worshiped, without argument, the fourteen Manus.

Now You are ours, O Brahman, O Supreme Soul of the wise! The universe is destroyed by the fear of Rudra; You are the destination of the fearless.

Chant this, may it be well with you, O pure sons of the King! Performing your own duty, with minds offered to the Lord.

Worship, praise, and meditate on that very Self situated in the self, situated in all beings—Hari.

Accepting the order of yoga, observing the vow of silence, holding. With concentrated minds, all of you practice this with respect.

This the Lord, the Master of the creators of the universe, spoke to us in the past. Desiring to create, to the sons like Bhṛgu who were desiring to create.

We, all the Prajāpatis, urged by that in the creation of progeny. By this, our darkness destroyed, we desire to create various progeny.

Now, a person constantly engaged in chanting this with attention. He quickly attains the ultimate good, devoted to Vāsudeva.

Of all benedictions here, knowledge is the supreme beatitude. He crosses the ocean of misery, difficult to cross, on the boat of knowledge, happily.

He who studies this hymn to the Lord sung by me with faith. He worships Hari, who is difficult to worship.

A person obtains from Him whatever he desires quickly. From Him, the only beloved of benedictions, who is well-pleased by the song sung by me.

A mortal who, rising at dawn, with folded hands and endowed with faith. Hears or causes to hear this, is released from the bondages of karma.

This hymn of the Supreme Person, the Supreme Soul, sung by me, O sons of the King! Chanting with one-pointed minds, perform great austerity; at the end, you will obtain the desired from Him.
